data.title: Veralto Non-GAAP EPS of $1.07 beats by $0.06, revenue of $1.42B beats by $20M
data.description:
• Veralto press release (VLTO): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.07 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $1.42B (+6.8% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • Operating cash flow was $182 million and non-GAAP free cash flow was $170 million
  • Capital allocation of ~$1 billion year-to-date:
    • Completed strategic acquisitions of In-Situ and GlobalVision(1) for ~$620 million
    • Repurchased $300 million shares, or 1.3% of outstanding shares(2)
  • Initiated cost optimization program(1) to streamline business processes and enhance operating efficiency:
    • Expect to incur a charge of $85 to $105 million and yield annual savings of $65 to $75 million by 2028

data.title: American Assets Trust FFO of $0.51 beats by $0.01, revenue of $110.59M beats by $0.81M
data.description:
• American Assets Trust press release (AAT): Q1 FFO of $0.51 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $110.59M (+1.8% Y/Y) beats by $0.81M.
  • The company affirms its guidance range for full year 2026 FFO per diluted share of $1.96 to $2.10 per share, with a midpoint of $2.03 vs consensus of $2.03.

data.title: Booking Holdings lowers FY26 outlook to reflect Middle East conflict; travel peers slump
data.description:
The conflict in the Middle East and its impact on the travel industry are weighing on shares of Booking Holdings (BKNG) as the company lowered its full-year outlook to reflect the expectations for disruptions to major transit corridors.

“We are assuming the direct and indirect impact from the conflict in the Middle East continues through the end of June,” the company said, adding that broader inflationary pressures, including fluctuations in jet fuel prices and airline capacity reductions, will also weigh on traveler sentiment “more broadly.”

“These dynamics can create headwinds across the travel value chain, and we are monitoring them closely. Our second quarter and full-year guidance are based on estimates and the information available to us at this time, in an unusually unpredictable environment.”

For the current quarter, Booking Holdings (BKNG) expects 2% to 4% room nights growth and gross bookings growth of 4% to 6%. Revenue and adjusted EBITDA are also expected to increase by 4% to 6%, translating into a range of $7.07B and $7.21B in revenue versus $7.53B estimates.

For FY26, gross bookings are expected to increase by high single digits to low single digits versus prior guidance for an increase of low double digits. Adjusted EPS is expected to increase by low to mid-teens versus a prior outlook of mid-teens, while revenue is expected to increase by high single digits compared to earlier guidance of low double digits.

For Q1, Booking Holdings (BKNG) earned a profit of $1.14 per share, beating expectations by $0.12, while revenue increased more than 16% to $5.53B, $10M better than anticipated.

These results reflect a 6% increase in room nights booked and a 15% gain in gross bookings, both of which were negatively impacted by approximately 2 percentage points by the conflict in the Middle East.

The company also announced a quarterly dividend of $0.42 per share, payable on June 30 to shareholders of record as of June 5.

Bookings (BKNG) results are weighing on the broader travel sector as well, with shares of Expedia (EXPE) and Airbnb (ABNB) lower in after-hours trading.

data.title: O-I Glass Non-GAAP EPS of $0.05 misses by $0.07, revenue of $1.54B beats by $70M
data.description:
• O-I Glass press release (OI): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.05 misses by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $1.54B (-1.9% Y/Y) beats by $70M.
  • Shipments declined 8%2, but trends improved through the quarter with March volumes down 2%
  • Revised guidance, primarily to reflect incremental energy-cost inflation. Energy management practices areexpected to limit further exposure and cover 75–80% of 2026 EU gas needs.
  • Shares -22%.

data.title: Markel reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Markel press release (MKL): Q1 Revenue of $3.55B (flat Y/Y) misses by $80M.
  • Operating loss, which includes market movements in our equity portfolio, was $273 million for the quarter.
  • Adjusted operating income, which excludes market movements in our equity portfolio, was $498 million for the quarter, which represents a 4% increase compared to the first quarter of 2025.

data.title: Mondelez International trades higher after Q1 results top expectations
data.description:
Mondelez International (MDLZ) swung higher in postmarket trading after the food giant reported fourth-quarter results.

Organic sales were up 3.0% during the quarter to smash the consensus estimate of +0.3%, led by an 11.3% rise for the Asia, Middle East & Africa segment and a 5.1% gain for the Latin America segment. Organic sales were up 0.6% for the North American business. Overall, pricing was up 0.9% in Q1, and volume/mix was down 0.5%.

Total revenue was up 8.3% year-over-year to $10.1B to top the consensus estimate by $340M.

The Chicago-based company's adjusted gross profit margin decreased 270 basis points to 30.7% of sales, driven primarily by higher input cost inflation and unfavorable volume/mix, partially offset by higher pricing and lower manufacturing costs driven by productivity.

Adjusted EPS was down 14.9% year over year to $0.67 vs. $0.73 consensus and $0.68 a year ago. The EPS decrease was driven by operating declines and higher income taxes, partially offset by lower interest and other expenses and fewer shares outstanding.

Looking ahead, Mondelez (MDLZ) sees organic revenue growth in the range of 0% to 2% for the full year and adjusted EPS growth of 0% to 5% on a constant currency basis. The company also expects 2026 free cash flow of $3B.

"The fundamentals of our business remain strong, the capabilities of our people are unmatched, and we continue to boldly invest behind our long-term growth opportunities to enable sustained performance for years to come," summarized CEO Dirk Van de Put.

Shares of Mondelez International (MDLZ) were up 1.7% in postmarket trading after gaining 2.0% during the regular session.

data.title: Equity Residential FFO of $0.99 beats by $0.03, rental income of $779.85M
data.description:
• Equity Residential press release (EQR): Q1 FFO of $0.99 beats by $0.03.
  • Rental income of $779.85M (+2.5% Y/Y).

  • The Company has established guidance ranges for the second quarter of 2026 EPS, FFO per share and Normalized FFO per share as listed below:

    Q2 2026Guidance

    EPS

    $0.28 to $0.32

    FFO per share

    $0.97 to $1.01

    Normalized FFO per share
